answer:
\boxed{B}  

The national vaccination coverage was 93% in August 2015, nearly identical to 94% in November 2014, indicating no substantial drop in overall coverage. Although the proportion of refusals doubled from 6% to 12%, this increase was small in absolute terms and did not significantly reduce the overall coverage rate. Therefore, the boycott call did not have a meaningful impact on the campaign’s overall vaccination coverage.
